content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 3, 1994

Brian M. Libow
City Attorney
City of San Pablo
One Alvarado Square
San Pablo, CA 94806

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-171

Dear Mr. Libow:

I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
May 17, 1994, wherein we discussed the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ pertaining to
Councilmember Brown.

Councilmember Brown is a real estate broker. A real estate
agent who works in Councilmember Brown's real estate office
represents a buyer who will be an applicant before the city
council. Councilmember Brown is willing to forego any percentage
of the commission income resulting from the transaction.

As we discussed, Regulation 18704.3(c) states that the
sources of commission income for a real estate broker include:

(A) The person the broker represents in the
transaction;
(B) If the broker receives a commission from a
transaction conducted by an agent working under the
broker's auspices, the person represented by the agent;
(C) Any brokerage business entity through which the
broker conducts business; and
(D) Any person who receives a finder's or other
referral fee for referring a party to the transaction to
the broker, or who makes a referral pursuant to a
contract with the broker.

Therefore, if Councilmember Brown waives any commission from
the agent's transaction with the buyer/applicant, she has no

___________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-171
Page 2


actual or promised income which may give rise to a conflict of
interest when the buyer is an applicant before the city council.
As I suggested, it would be prudent for Councilmember Brown to
document in writing her intention to forego this commission
income.

I trust this answers your question.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jil Stecher
Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JS:ak

sections.conclusion: Therefore, if Councilmember Brown waives any commission from the agent's transaction with the buyer/applicant, she has no actual or promised income which may give rise to a conflict of interest when the buyer is an applicant before the city council. As I suggested, it would be prudent for Councilmember Brown to document in writing her intention to forego this commission income.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether Councilmember Brown, a real estate broker, has a conflict of interest when a real estate agent working in her office represents a buyer who will appear before the city council, if the councilmember waives her commission from that transaction.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No conflict of interest exists if Councilmember Brown waives her commission from the agent's transaction, since she would have no actual or promised income from the matter. The FPPC recommends she document this waiver in writing.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit 'QUESTION' and 'CONCLUSION' section headers but contains these elements in narrative form. Response letter from FPPC General Counsel confirming telephone conversation advice. Regulation 18704.3(c) cited as interpretive authority.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Councilmember Brown, a real estate broker, has a conflict of interest when a real estate agent working in her office represents a buyer who will appear before the city council, if the councilmember waives her commission from that transaction.

CONCLUSION: Therefore, if Councilmember Brown waives any commission from the agent's transaction with the buyer/applicant, she has no actual or promised income which may give rise to a conflict of interest when the buyer is an applicant before the city council. As I suggested, it would be prudent for Councilmember Brown to document in writing her intention to forego this commission income.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Councilmember Brown, a real estate broker, may avoid a conflict of interest by waiving her commission when an agent in her office represents a buyer appearing before the city council. The FPPC recommends written documentation of the waiver.
